title: Steven Schick

infobox.value: This article on a percussionist is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Steven_Schick&action=edit).

(born May 8, 1954[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is a [percussionist](/wiki/Percussion "Percussion") and conductor from the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), specializing in [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music"). He teaches at the [University of California, San Diego](/wiki/University_of_California,_San_Diego "University of California, San Diego") and was the music director and conductor of the La Jolla Symphony Orchestra from 2007 to 2022.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American musician

**Steven Schick** (born May 8, 1954[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is a [percussionist](/wiki/Percussion "Percussion") and conductor from the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), specializing in [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music"). He teaches at the [University of California, San Diego](/wiki/University_of_California,_San_Diego "University of California, San Diego") and was the music director and conductor of the La Jolla Symphony Orchestra from 2007 to 2022.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In February 2011, Schick was named director of the [San Francisco Contemporary Music Players](/wiki/San_Francisco_Contemporary_Music_Players "San Francisco Contemporary Music Players"). He replaces Maestro [David Milnes](/wiki/David_Milnes "David Milnes"), who stepped down in 2009.

In 2012, he became the first artist-in-residence with the [International Contemporary Ensemble](/wiki/International_Contemporary_Ensemble "International Contemporary Ensemble") (ICE).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** ["Steven Schick, Artist in Residence (2012-2015)"](http://iceorg.org/about/artist/schick). International Contemporary Ensemble. 2011. Retrieved 22 February 2015.
